news.title: Last chance to apply — Startup Battlefield Australia applications close July 6
news.summary: If you're going to apply for Startup Battlefield Australia, now is the time. Applications close July 6, and once the deadline passes, the opportunity is gone.

news.title: Mark Zuckerberg tells staff that AI agents haven’t progressed as quickly as he’d hoped
news.summary: At an internal meeting, the Meta CEO reportedly said that AI development efforts were not moving as quickly as anticipated.

news.title: Private space pilots are flying orbital missions for the US Space Force
news.summary: True Anomaly and Rocket Lab are performing Top Gun-style satellite fly-bys for the U.S. military.

news.title: Thiel Capital’s Jack Selby nabs stakes in hot startups like Etched through Arizona connections
news.summary: Selby's VC firm, Copper Sky Capital, is currently raising a $300 million second fund, according to a regulatory filing.

news.title: IQM, Europe’s first public quantum company, admits the future of the tech is uncertain
news.summary: IQM, a full-stack quantum company out of Finland, went public on the Nasdaq today at a valuation of about $1.9 billion.

news.title: Sony’s PlayStation disc factory is already being repurposed
news.summary: The video game disc is dead, and Sony's been planning to kill it for some time, according to a report out of Austria. news.title: Tesla driver faces manslaughter charges over Texas crash that killed a woman inside her home

news.title: Meta has a new app called Pocket that is absolutely nothing like the old Pocket
news.summary: Mozilla shut down the well-loved read-it-later Pocket app last year, and now Meta is launching an app called Pocket with an entirely different, AI-focused pitch: this new app lets you make and share little interactive "gizmos" built from an AI prompt, as reported by Business Insider. news.title: Newly discovered PamStealer isn't your typical macOS malware
news.summary: The discovery underscores the increased effort being poured into Mac infostealers.

news.title: FAA proposal: Supersonic airliners can fly over US cities if they’re quiet
news.summary: New US rules would legalize quiet supersonic flights without the sonic boom.

news.title: Ars Live recap: When are the big rockets NASA desperately needs going to be ready?
news.summary: I have not seen anyone put out a date for a new rocket, and actually hit it.

news.title: Plex debuts 5-year membership pass for $250
news.summary: Plex is pushing customers to newer features and more frequent payments.

news.title: Africa CDC confirms Marburg case in Uganda as Ebola outbreak rages
news.summary: Early reports indicate there may be another case, but spread is thought to be localized.

news.title: The Onion’s ‘Infowars’ Parody Is Here. Alex Jones Is Going to Hate It
news.summary: The satirical site is fighting to officially take over Infowars. In the meantime, CEO Ben Collins says the new show will mock “how fucking stupid” conspiratorial brain rot has become.

news.title: How Big Is ‘Love Island USA’? More Than 10 Million People Are Already on Its App
news.summary: “We have more people voting on the ‘Love Island USA’ app than we do in many political elections taking place across the country,” says the show's executive producer.

news.title: Spotify Confirms Streaming Fraud After Kalshi Trader Cries Foul
news.summary: One of Kalshi’s most prominent traders tells WIRED he’s swearing off Spotify-related markets until the issue is resolved.

news.title: Can Cursor Remain a Platform for OpenAI and Anthropic’s Models Inside SpaceX?
news.summary: Cursor hopes to continue offering third-party AI models after it's acquired by SpaceX, testing the relationships between frontier AI labs.

news.title: The DEA Plans to Ban Opioid-Like Kratom Compound 7-OH
news.summary: The federal agency says it will temporarily schedule the drug, which has been called “gas station heroin,” as a controlled substance—a boon for MAHA and the mainstream kratom industry.
